job_description:
Sr. B2B Content Specialist

2Base Technologies is at a deliberate stage of building its marketing function, and this role owns the written voice of that function end to end. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not invent the voice. It operationalises, applies, and enforces it across all written output: long-form content that earns search traffic, content written in the voice of senior leadership for professional publishing, and the quality standard for everything written that leaves this company.

The audience spans technical leaders and business decision-makers at mid-market companies. CTOs and IT Directors who evaluate architecture decisions, and Founders, COOs, and Operations leaders who are accountable for technology outcomes without always having a technical background. Writing that works across that range, without oversimplifying for one end or losing the other, is the actual craft challenge of this role. Technically credible, commercially relevant, and written with a clear point of view. That is the standard this role is hired to meet and maintain.

What You Will Own

  • All long-form written content: pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, and use-case libraries targeting senior decision-makers in the US and UK
  • Written content produced in the voice of senior leadership for professional platforms, requiring the ability to write convincingly as someone other than yourself
  • The 2Base content voice, including auditing all published content to ensure it meets the company's brand standards
  • Content briefs written clearly enough that a junior team member can produce a first draft without needing to ask clarifying questions
  • The content quality gate: nothing is published without passing through this role first
  • AI tool usage as a productivity layer, not as a substitute for thinking

What You Will Not Own

  • SEO keyword selection: the SEO Specialist sets this and you write within it
  • Social media scheduling and publishing: a separate team member owns this
  • Visual design of content assets: the Visual Designer owns this
  • Distribution strategy: the Marketing & Growth Lead decides which channels content goes to
  • ICP definition: you write for the ICP and you do not define it

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years writing B2B content, with strong candidates typically at the 5 to 6 year mark - Has written content that ranked on Google, not just content that was published - Has written in voices other than their own, for executives, founders, or senior professionals - Understands SEO at a working level: knows what search intent means and can write to it without being an SEO specialist - Has worked with AI writing tools and knows both their value and their limits - Can show content that contributed to a measurable outcome: traffic, leads, or engagement with the right audience

Background - Technology, SaaS, or professional services background strongly preferred

Writing Ability - Writes with a clear point of view, not neutral summaries of other people's ideas - Can explain complex technical concepts in plain language without losing technical credibility - Writes concisely with no filler, no padding, and no unnecessary qualification - Can write in different voices and make each one sound authentic, not just adjusted

Mindset - Treats content as a commercial tool, not a creative exercise - Comfortable being edited and does not treat feedback as personal criticism - Genuinely curious about AI, technology, and business operations, not faking interest to get the job - Fast: this team moves at pace and cannot wait three days for a first draft

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios of "Top 10 Tips" articles or trend summaries with no original thinking
  • Writers who cannot explain how any of their content performed or what it contributed to
  • Anyone who has never written for a technical or senior business audience and considers that detail irrelevant
  • Writers who only produce self-directed content and have never worked consistently to a brief
  • Anyone who cannot write convincingly in a voice other than their own
  • Candidates who treat AI tools as the primary author and apply only light editing

Benefits

A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior B2B Content Specialist required to craft technically credible, commercially relevant content for technical and business leaders. 3-5 years of B2B content experience with SEO and AI tool proficiency needed.

job_description:
Sr. Visual Designer at 2Base Technologies

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from scratch, and this role makes it look like a company worth taking seriously. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

Before a CTO reads a word of our content, they see the design. Before they trust the argument, they have already made a judgment about whether this company looks credible. This role owns that first impression and every asset that follows. The visual function at 2Base is being built from the ground up with no existing brand system, template library, or consistent visual identity. Your priority in the first 30 days is to build the brand system, which will serve as the foundation for all subsequent assets like LinkedIn carousels, ad creatives, landing pages, and presentation templates.

This is not a creative agency role. The audience is skeptical, senior, and technically literate. Design that looks clever but reads as unclear, or feels consumer-facing rather than professional, will not work with this ICP. Restraint, precision, and consistency matter more than visual novelty.

What You Will Own

  • The 2Base visual brand system (colour palette, typography, spacing, layout rules, and documentation)
  • Marketing and content visual assets (LinkedIn carousels, social graphics, infographics, diagrams)
  • Paid advertising creatives (Google Display, LinkedIn Ads) designed for testing and iteration
  • Landing page visual design (layout, components, conversion-focused structure)
  • Email, presentation, and sales deck templates
  • A template library for independent on-brand asset creation
  • Design consistency auditing
  • Secondary responsibility: light video editing and motion for social reels, YouTube, and website use

What You Will Not Own

  • Message, argument, or information hierarchy (owned by the Content and Thought Leadership Specialist)
  • Copywriting (owned by the Content Specialist)
  • Website development (owned by the development team)
  • Content strategy or brand positioning

Requirements

  • Minimum 3 to 5 years in a B2B design role (technology, SaaS, or professional services preferred)
  • Experience building or evolving a brand system
  • LinkedIn design experience (carousels, document posts, single-image content)
  • Paid ad creative experience with testing/iteration
  • Landing page design with conversion understanding
  • Basic video editing for social/web content
  • Proficiency in Figma (non-negotiable) and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects)
  • Portfolio demonstrating clean, structured design with clear reasoning
  • Ability to explain design decisions
  • Audience-focused design (not general appeal)
  • Strong typography skills
  • Credible B2B design at first glance

Mindset

  • Works from structured briefs
  • Maintains speed without sacrificing quality
  • Treats design as a communication tool
  • Open to feedback and constructive critique

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios dominated by consumer brands, lifestyle work, or event design
  • Candidates who cannot justify design decisions
  • Designers without brand system-building experience
  • Overcomplicated visuals with excessive fonts/colors/styles
  • Candidates requiring complete creative freedom

Benefits

  • Growth-driven environment with leadership, innovation, and upskilling opportunities

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior Visual Designer to build brand system and create B2B marketing assets with precision and restraint.

job_description:
QA Engineer

We are seeking a detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of hands-on experience in software testing to join our QA team. job_description:
About the Role

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role, not a screen updater role, and not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modeling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will be the functional brain of the project. You will ensure that the right problem is understood, broken down, analyzed, validated, clarified, communicated, and owned end-to-end. You will drive requirement precision, reduce rework, and enable confident and aligned delivery across teams.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly.
  • Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement.
  • Convert ambiguous client inputs into structured, logical, and actionable functional understanding.
  • Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies.
  • Build strong system-level understanding involving APIs, integrations, data flow, and backend considerations.
  • Translate business processes into functional logic that development teams can execute.
  • Decompose requirements into use-case flows, system behaviors, edge cases, alternate paths, validation rules, and dependencies.
  • Prepare high-quality documentation, including BRD, FRD, SRS, workflows, state diagrams, data-flow diagrams, API requirement specs, and acceptance criteria.
  • Ensure all documentation is clear, logical, structured, testable, and ready for developers and QA to use.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product owners to validate feasibility and align understanding.
  • Ensure QA teams can derive test cases effectively from your functional logic and acceptance criteria.
  • Communicate confidently with clients, handle requirement discussions smoothly, and clarify misunderstandings immediately.
  • Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly.
  • Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development.
  • Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability.
  • Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 4+ years of core Business Analyst experience with strong analytical and system-level thinking.
  • Ability to break down complex requirements into clear components, workflows, and functional logic.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication with confidence in client interactions and clarifications.
  • Proven experience in preparing SRS, BRD, FRD, workflows, and acceptance criteria with high clarity.
  • Ability to challenge assumptions politely, ask the right questions, and ensure zero ambiguity.
  • Strong collaboration experience with development, QA, architects, and cross-functional teams.
  • Understanding of API behaviors, integrations, data flows, and multi-module system interactions.
  • Ability to run requirement discussions, workshops, and independently lead discovery sessions.
  • Exposure to SaaS, scalable products, automation, or AI-supported systems is an added advantage.
  • Demonstrated ownership of requirements end-to-end, ensuring developer-ready and testable outputs.
  • Not suitable for UI-only thinkers, passive communicators, template-based writers, or those relying on AI for answers.
  • Success is defined by clear, testable requirements, zero rework, strong cross-team alignment, and confident communication.
  • Candidates must align with 2Base’s structured thinking, clarity-driven culture, and transformation of the BA function.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or related field
  • 4 to 8 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst
  • Experience in SaaS or scalable product environments
  • Exposure to AI/Automation, integrations, or multi-module systems
  • Ability to run independent client workshops
  • Must have independently handled requirement ownership for at least one full project lifecycle

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior Business Analyst role focused on functional modeling, requirement clarity, and cross-team alignment. Own end-to-end requirements, drive precision, and enable confident delivery of scalable systems.

job_description:
